1_verify_vertex_auth_path.correction_applied: The auth path was already healthy on GKE. The remaining blocker was that travel runtime defaults pointed vertex_project/gcp_project at noetl-cluster instead of the enabled GCP project noetl-demo-19700101. ops#60 and docs#51 fixed the default, then travel runtime was registered as version 2 on the GKE server.

flights_status.note: Amadeus still returned HTTP 500, so the expected travel widget is the friendly-error app:column; this is separate from Vertex provider validation.

locations_status.note: Amadeus still returned HTTP 500, so the expected travel widget is the friendly-error app:column; this is separate from Vertex provider validation.

notes: GKE credentials were obtained with gcloud container clusters get-credentials for noetl-cluster in us-central1, project noetl-demo-19700101.
Worker pod metadata auth is valid: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS is unset, metadata server returns noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com and a bearer access token.

The previous AMBER was not caused by missing GKE Vertex auth; the travel playbook targeted vertex_project=noetl-cluster. Vertex AI returned HTTP 403 CONSUMER_INVALID for that project string. Fixing the default to noetl-demo-19700101 made the existing Workload Identity path pass.
The GKE travel runtime is registered as catalog 623381857714832176 version 2 after ops#60.
Amadeus test API 500s remain a separate upstream issue; the travel runtime correctly renders friendly-error widgets while preserving effective_provider=vertex-ai.
deferred_followups: Investigate Amadeus test API 500s on flights/locations
